text.content:
Lake Monster

Reward: 5 cp per knucklehead trout caught (from Grynsk); spell scroll of animal friendship + 5 gp (from Tali, if notes taken)

The characters learn that an unknown creature in Maer Dualdon has been attacking and sinking Bremen fishing boats. In truth, a frost druid named Ravisin recently cast awaken on a plesiosaurus, granting it intelligence and speech, and instructed it to attack Bremen fishers to drive them away.

Getting the Quest

Near the frozen docks, the characters encounter Grynsk Berylbore, a grizzled shield dwarf (commoner) who owns two rowboats: the Burly Ram and the Pronged Goat. The Pronged Goat has a large bite taken out of its gunwale. Grynsk mistakes the characters for fishers he hired the night before and immediately puts them to work at 5 cp per trout. He lies about the damage, claiming the boat hit an ice floe.

"Good, you're here. Get to it, ingrates! Those fish ain't gonna catch themselves!"

A DC 11 Wisdom (Insight) check reveals Grynsk is lying. Pressed, he becomes annoyed and tells them to get off his docks.

Regardless, Tali (neutral good half-elf scout, they/them) appears and warns the characters about the lake monster. Tali is in Icewind Dale studying how climate changes affect wildlife and has been investigating the attacks. Tali gives the characters a notebook and asks them to record everything they observe about the creature.

Unsafe Waters

The characters may use Grynsk's two rowboats (AC 11, 50 hp each; the damaged one has 36 hp). Each boat holds up to 4 Medium creatures and their gear, speed 1.5 mph.

The lake is covered in ice floes. As characters row out, they must steer around 1d4+1 ice floes. DC 14 Wisdom check (add proficiency if proficient with water vehicles) or the boat takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage.

Falling in: Frigid water — Constitution score in minutes before exhaustion sets in. DC 10 Con save each additional minute or gain one level of exhaustion.

At the end of each hour, roll on the Bremen: Lake Events table.

The Clever Beast

When the plesiosaurus appears, read:

Ripples disrupt the surface of the lake, causing your boat to bob from side to side. A large mass glides through the dark water below you.

DC 13 Intelligence (Nature) to identify it as a plesiosaurus. Roll on the Bremen: Plesiosaurus Behavior table.

The plesiosaurus is awakened and can speak Common. To get it talking, characters must cast message or similar magic, or yell loudly while it's nearby. If it surfaces and is spoken to kindly:

"A reptilian head at the end of a long, slender neck rises out of the cold, dark water. The creature gazes at you intently and says, in Common, 'I'm listening.'"

What it knows:

  • "I used to just swim around the lake and eat fish."
  • "The human who gave me this gift is named Ravisin and claimed to serve the Frostmaiden. Ravisin said I must do my part to help the Frostmaiden, who wants the people of Bremen to suffer."
  • "I don't want to be a stupid beast again." (It doesn't know the awakening is permanent.)

DC 14 Charisma (Persuasion) to get it to reveal Ravisin instructed it to spread fear among Bremen fishers.
DC 18 Charisma (Persuasion) to convince it the awakening is permanent — on success, it promises to stop attacking.

DC 15 Intelligence (Arcana) to conclude it's the beneficiary of a permanent awaken spell.

If attacked, the plesiosaurus dives and attacks boats from below. Below half hit points, it disengages and flees.

Concluding the Quest

Return to Grynsk: he pays 5 cp per trout caught.
Return to Tali with notes: spell scroll of animal friendship + 5 gp.
No notes: no reward from Tali.

Note: Ravisin the frost druid can be encountered later in "The White Moose" quest in Lonelywood.
